behaviors.description: Generated function signature matches expected interface from the NL description.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Agentic, because this behavior is about whether the LLM actually produces the correct signature (names/types/return type), which is model-output quality. Pass 2 downgrade check: Integration could test signature-validation plumbing with stubs, but that would not test the stated behavior "generated function signature matches" under real model variability. Upgrade check: Workflow is not required because this behavior is satisfied on a single generation step; no multi-step agent pipeline is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service with a real LLM on a set of interface-sensitive prompts; assert the emitted function signature exactly matches expected parameter names, parameter types, and return type. Repeat runs to catch non-deterministic signature drift.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Real model output is required to catch signature-quality failures; lower levels mostly validate plumbing.

behaviors.description: Generated implementation passes all provided tests on first or subsequent attempt.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Workflow, because the behavior explicitly includes iterative success ("first or a subsequent attempt"), which can require multiple LLM generations plus test feedback across steps. Pass 2 downgrade check: Agentic would cover one real model step, but does not fully exercise the multi-step success path explicitly named. Upgrade check: no higher level exists. Rejection-gate for lower level: the text "first or a subsequent attempt" disqualifies single-step-only levels as sufficient minima.
behaviors.test_description: Use real LLM calls and the real service loop on tasks where first attempt may fail; verify at least one case passes after refinement and that final status is success only when tests actually pass.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires chained agentic steps, so Workflow is the minimum that matches the behavior.

behaviors.description: On test failure, refinement prompt includes failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because the failure mode is at a local module boundary: test runner output must be handed to prompt-construction/orchestration. Pass 2 downgrade check: Unit is possible only if prompt assembly is isolated; but behavior is specifically about cross-module handoff from failed tests to next prompt. Upgrade check: System not required by stated text; no requirement for real subprocess/network behavior, only that failure messages are included. Agentic not required because correctness does not depend on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator + test-result provider + prompt builder with in-process fakes; trigger a failed test result and assert the exact failure messages appear in the subsequent LLM request payload.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration-brake passed: no explicit OS/network requirement, no real-AI-quality requirement, no multi-agentic chain correctness criterion.

behaviors.description: Service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns structured error containing all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, since this is orchestration/state aggregation across generator, tester, and error formatter modules over multiple attempts. Pass 2 downgrade check: Unit could verify a pure loop counter, but the stated behavior requires collecting per-attempt test results into a structured error across component handoffs. Upgrade check: System is not required by behavior text; it does not require real subprocess/OS semantics, only correct local control flow and aggregation. Agentic not required because failures can be deterministically simulated.
behaviors.test_description: Use deterministic in-process stubs that fail tests on every attempt; assert exactly 3 attempts occur, no 4th call is made, and returned error schema includes attempt 1..3 with corresponding test-result payloads.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Chosen test exercises cross-module state propagation; lower-level pure-function tests would miss handoff regressions.

behaviors.description: End-to-end loop (generate, test, refine, retest) yields a working implementation for a function usually missed by one LLM call.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Workflow, because behavior explicitly requires a multi-step agentic sequence and improvement across steps. Pass 2 downgrade check: Agentic is too low because a single real model call cannot validate the full iterative loop named in the behavior. Upgrade check: none above Workflow. Rejection-gate for lower levels: the phrase "full loop — generate, test, refine, test again" directly disqualifies single-step levels.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real pipeline with a benchmark prompt known to be hard for one-shot generation; verify one-shot baseline fails, then iterative loop succeeds within allowed attempts with passing tests at the end.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Behavior is inherently multi-step and model-dependent; Workflow is required.
